questions.text: What is 'computer use' in the context of the Claude Agent SDK?

options.label: A
options.text: A feature that lets Claude write Python scripts for computation

options.label: B
options.text: A capability allowing Claude to interact with a desktop GUI by taking screenshots and simulating mouse/keyboard input

options.label: C
options.text: An API for accessing cloud computing resources

options.label: D
options.text: A tool for monitoring CPU and memory usage

questions.explanation: Computer use is a Claude capability that allows it to interact with a desktop environment — taking screenshots to perceive the screen state and simulating mouse clicks and keyboard input to control applications.

questions.text: How can you implement multi-agent workflows with the Claude Agent SDK?

options.label: A
options.text: By running multiple Claude processes on separate machines

options.label: B
options.text: By using one Claude agent as an orchestrator that calls other specialized agents as tools

options.label: C
options.text: Multi-agent workflows are not supported

options.label: D
options.text: By using threading to run agents in parallel without coordination

questions.explanation: Multi-agent workflows can be built by having an orchestrator agent that delegates subtasks to specialized subagents by calling them as tools. The orchestrator receives results from subagents and coordinates the overall task.

questions.text: What is the purpose of streaming in the Claude Agent SDK?

options.label: A
options.text: To stream video content to users

options.label: B
options.text: To reduce API costs by sending smaller payloads

options.label: C
options.text: To progressively deliver Claude's response tokens as they are generated, improving perceived latency

options.label: D
options.text: To compress the model's output for faster transmission

questions.explanation: Streaming allows you to receive Claude's response incrementally as tokens are generated rather than waiting for the full response. This significantly improves perceived latency in interactive applications.

questions.text: What is 'prompt caching' in the Claude Agent SDK and what does it optimize?

options.label: A
options.text: Caching the final answer so identical questions are answered without calling the API

options.label: B
options.text: Storing reusable prefix portions of the prompt on Anthropic's servers to reduce token processing costs on repeated calls

options.label: C
options.text: Saving user prompts to a local database for offline use

options.label: D
options.text: Compressing the prompt to reduce its token count

questions.explanation: Prompt caching allows you to mark stable portions of your prompt (like a large system prompt or documents) with cache_control. Anthropic stores these on their servers so subsequent calls with the same prefix don't reprocess those tokens, reducing cost and latency.

questions.text: In an advanced Claude agent, what is the recommended strategy for handling tool execution errors?

options.label: A
options.text: Crash the agent immediately on any tool error

options.label: B
options.text: Silently ignore all tool errors

options.label: C
options.text: Return the error as a tool_result so Claude can reason about the failure and try an alternative approach

options.label: D
options.text: Automatically retry the tool 10 times before giving up

questions.explanation: The recommended pattern is to return error information back to Claude as a tool_result. This lets Claude understand what went wrong and reason about alternative approaches, retry with different parameters, or inform the user appropriately.
